ID:       fizeau-61668fd3
Title:    Rename Fizeau native harness identity from agent to fiz
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:build, area:contract, area:harness, area:rename, kind:breaking, downstream:ddx, triage:needs-investigation
Parent:   fizeau-06524f43
Desc:     PROBLEM
DDx is removing the term `agent` from its public and internal task-execution surfaces except where it literally means an external AI agent. Fizeau still exposes its native execution path as harness `agent` in code, tests, docs, progress fixtures, and contract prose. That makes downstream DDx specs confusing because `agent` can mean the old DDx CLI namespace, Fizeau native loop, or a real external coding agent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The public contract and service request still document native-only behavior as Harness=="agent" in service.go:609-620. CONTRACT-003 selection docs at docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md:424-437 still describe hard `Harness` pins but do not reserve `agent` for real external agents or name the native Fizeau harness as `fiz`. Historical rename planning in docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-08-rename-agent.md:143-164 discusses temporary aliases, but the downstream DDx decision for this work is remove, not deprecate.

PROPOSED FIX
Make `fiz` the public/native Fizeau harness identity across CONTRACT-003, the root facade comments, native harness registration, CLI/help text, route-status/list-models output, tests, and committed cassettes/fixtures as needed. Remove public `agent` native-harness aliases rather than adding deprecation shims. Keep the word `agent` only where it refers to a real external AI agent concept, historical artifact text that cannot be changed safely, or third-party standards language.

NON-SCOPE
Do not rename DDx commands or DDx packages in this repo. Do not alter routing scoring, provider eligibility, quota behavior, or model catalog contents except where tests/fixtures must be renamed from `agent` to `fiz`. Do not add a compatibility alias for `agent` unless a later bead explicitly changes the no-deprecation policy.

In-scope files include:
- service.go
- service_models.go and service_routing.go/native harness registration call sites as needed
- agentcli/ and cmd/fiz/ help or command text if they expose native harness names
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md
- docs/helix/02-design/primary-harness-capability-baseline.md
- tests/fixtures/cassettes that assert `agent` as the native harness identity

Out-of-scope files:
- DDx repository files under ../ddx
- benchmark result archives unless an active test fixture requires the rename

ID:       fizeau-400b57fe
Title:    Define Fizeau-owned fuzzy model constraint resolution
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:build, area:contract, area:routing, area:model-catalog, kind:feature, downstream:ddx, triage:no-changes-unverified
Parent:   fizeau-06524f43
Desc:     PROBLEM
DDx needs to expose `--model <value>` as an operator escape hatch for routing failures and experimentation, but DDx must pass the raw value unchanged and must not implement model aliases, normalization, fuzzy matching, catalog lookup, or ambiguity handling. Fizeau therefore needs CONTRACT-003 to define how explicit model constraints such as `qwen36` resolve against discovered/provider model IDs such as `Qwen-3.6-27b-MLX-8bit`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
Fizeau already has partial OpenAI-compatible provider matching in internal/provider/openai/discovery.go:135-155: it lowercases, strips the first slash prefix, and substring-matches the requested string. That is not yet the public contract, and it does not remove punctuation/special separators, so `qwen36` will not reliably match `Qwen-3.6-27b-MLX-8bit`. CONTRACT-003 currently says ModelRef concrete refs are exact at docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md:429-433, but it does not define explicit `Model` normalization/fuzzy behavior, ambiguity errors, or no-match evidence for the Execute path.

PROPOSED FIX
Amend CONTRACT-003 so Fizeau owns model constraint resolution for `ServiceExecuteRequest.Model` and `RouteRequest.Model`. The matching contract should normalize case, whitespace, and separators such as hyphen, underscore, dot, slash, colon, and other non-alphanumeric delimiters before substring matching; allow catalog aliases where present; resolve a single match to the provider/discovered canonical model ID; return a typed ambiguity error with candidates on multiple matches; and return a typed no-match error or existing provider error with nearby candidates/evidence when none match. Apply the same semantics consistently to Execute and ResolveRoute. DDx will pass raw `--model qwen36` through unchanged and record the actual model Fizeau reports.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add DDx-side normalization or matching. Do not change profile/ModelRef semantics except to clarify the difference from raw Model constraint resolution. Do not change route scoring or auto-routing eligibility except where exact/fuzzy explicit model constraints need candidate filtering. Do not make broad Levenshtein-style fuzzy guesses unless ambiguity/no-match behavior is explicitly test-covered.

In-scope files include:
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md
- service_routing.go and routing helper packages used by Execute/ResolveRoute
- internal/provider/openai/discovery.go if its matching primitive is generalized or reused
- routing_errors.go if typed ambiguity/no-match errors need public shape
- service_routing_errors_test.go, service_http_provider_test.go, internal/provider/openai/discovery_test.go, and public contract tests

Out-of-scope files:
- DDx repository files under ../ddx
- model catalog manifest content unless adding an alias fixture is necessary for tests

1	package agent
2	
3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
14		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can override one knob
34	// for a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			factory := serviceRunFactory
54			if factory == nil {
55				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
56			}
57			built, err := factory(projectRoot)
58			if err != nil {
59				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
60			}
61			svc = built
62		}
63		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
64	}
65	
66	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
67	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
68	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
69	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
70	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
71		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
72		if harness == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
73			harness = rcfg.Harness()
74		}
75		model := runtime.ModelOverride
76		if model == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
77			model = rcfg.Model()
78		}
79		modelRef := runtime.ModelRefOverride
80		if modelRef == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
81			modelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()

The primary Fizeau types imported via `agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"` are:

   - `/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go:444` — Asserts result.Harness == "agent"
   - `/passthrough_envelope_test.go:390` — Result harness validation
   - `/session_index_test.go:320` — Session index harness entry
   - `/models_test.go:15` — Harness list includes "agent"

### 3. **ServiceExecuteRequest Field Usage**

DDx populates the following fields when constructing ServiceExecuteRequest:

**From `/agent_runner_service.go:56-72`:**
- `Prompt` — prompt text
- `Model` — resolved model identifier
- `Provider` — optional explicit provider name
- `Harness` — hard-coded as "agent"
- `ModelRef` — opaque model reference
- `Reasoning` — mapped from effort level
- `Permissions` — safe/supervised/unrestricted
- `WorkDir` — working directory
- `Timeout` — wall-clock timeout
- `IdleTimeout` — idle (inactivity) timeout
- `ProviderTimeout` — provider request timeout
- `SessionLogDir` — session log directory
- `Metadata` — merged from correlation and env map
- `Role` — from options
- `CorrelationID` — from options

**From `/service_run.go:219-238` (more complete, includes new v0.10.0 fields):**
Also includes:
- `Profile` — routing intent (default/cheap/fast/smart)
- `MinPower` — minimum model power constraint
- `MaxPower` — maximum model power constraint

### 4. **How Model Strings are Passed to Fizeau**

Model strings flow through this resolution path:

1. **Explicit pin** — From RunArgs/ResolvedConfig Model field → ServiceExecuteRequest.Model
2. **Profile-driven routing** — When Model is empty but Profile is set, Fizeau routes within harness eligible models
3. **ModelRef** — Opaque identifier passed separately to Fizeau for resolution
4. **Result override** — Fizeau can return a different Model in ServiceRoutingActual if routing selected a different model than requested

This is noted in `/service_run.go:244-245`:
> "When Harness is set and Model is empty, fizeau routes within the harness's eligible models using Profile/MinPower as the routing constraint."

### 5. **How Execution Results (Model/Provider/Harness) are Recorded**

Results are captured and propagated through multiple layers:

**A. Result Struct (`/types.go:122-149`)** — Holds:
- `Harness` — from execution request or routing decision
- `Provider` — from routing decision or actual routing
- `Model` — from execution request or overridden by routing
- `ActualPower` — from RoutingActual
- `PredictedPower/SpeedTPS/CostUSDPer1kTokens/CostSource` — from routing candidate metrics
- `AgentSessionID` — session log path for cross-reference

**B. Result Population in `/service_run.go:262-321`**:

1. Initial Result constructed with request values:
   ```go
   result := &Result{
       Harness:    harness,
       Model:      model,
       DurationMS: int(elapsed.Milliseconds()),
   }
   ```

2. Updated from ServiceRoutingDecisionData (routing event, /service_run.go:267-280):
   - `result.Provider = routing.Provider`
   - `result.Model = routing.Model` (if non-empty)
   - `result.Harness = routing.Harness` (if non-empty)
   - Predicted metrics from winning eligible candidate

3. Updated from ServiceFinalData (final event, /service_run.go:282-321):
   - Output text
   - Token usage
   - Exit code and error
   - RoutingActual overrides (lines 304-317):
     ```go
     if final.RoutingActual != nil {
         if result.Provider == "" {
             result.Provider = final.RoutingActual.Provider
         }
         if final.RoutingActual.Model != "" {
             result.Model = final.RoutingActual.Model
         }
         if final.RoutingActual.Harness != "" {
             result.Harness = final.RoutingActual.Harness
         }
         if final.RoutingActual.Power > 0 {
             result.ActualPower = final.RoutingActual.Power
         }
     }
     ```

**C. Session Index Recording** (`/service_run.go:324-331`):
Results are converted to SessionIndexEntry and appended to session index file:
```go
entry := SessionIndexEntryFromResult(workDir, SessionIndexInputs{...}, result, start, finishedAt)
_ = AppendSessionIndex(ResolveLogDir(workDir, ""), entry, finishedAt)
```

**D. Routing Metrics Recording** (`/routing_metrics.go:67-110`):
The legacy Runner.recordRoutingOutcome method records:
- Harness, Surface, Model (canonical target)
- Success/Failure, Latency, Tokens, Cost
- Correlation IDs (trace_id, span_id, native_session_id, native_log_ref)

### Key Technical Points

1. **"agent" is special** — It's the DDx embedded harness, runs in-process via Fizeau (Binary="fiz"), distinct from subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, etc.)
2. **Two result paths**: Routing decision (predicted metrics) and final event (actual routing) separately populate Result struct with fallback precedence
3. **Metadata preservation** — Correlation IDs flow through to session index via `metadataWithEnv()` merging
4. **Power as routing metric** — New v0.10.0 feature: actual Power from RoutingActual stored in Result.ActualPower; predicted Power from ServiceRoutingDecisionData in Result.PredictedPower

ID:       fizeau-32e533bb
Title:    ddx-coord: bump fizeau pin to v0.11.0; drop --model-ref flags; rename Profile→Policy in DDx call sites
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:ddx-coord, kind:rename, phase:9, breaking, spec-id:ADR-009
Deps:     fizeau-264e70c4
Desc:     PROBLEM
  DDx (github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx) imports fizeau as a Go module and currently pins v0.10.x with field references to ServiceExecuteRequest.Profile, ServiceExecuteRequest.ModelRef, and CLI flag --model-ref in five files. After fizeau v0.11.0 ships with the breaking redesign, DDx CI is red until updated.

ROOT CAUSE
  DDx is the only known external consumer of the fizeau Go module. The breaking changes (Profile→Policy field rename, ModelRef field removal, error type renames, --model-ref CLI flag removal) require coordinated DDx-side updates.

DESIGN INTENT
  - Land DDx coordination as one PR pinned to fizeau v0.11.0 (not a pre-release).
  - Drop --model-ref flag from all DDx CLI subcommands (run, try, work, agent_execute_bead, agent_cmd).
  - Rename ServiceExecuteRequest{Profile, ModelRef} field assignments to {Policy} (drop ModelRef entirely).
  - Update DDx test stubs that mock fizeau service interface (ListProfiles → ListPolicies, drop ResolveProfile/ProfileAliases mocks).
  - Update DDx-internal wire-translation point at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go to set fiz Policy field from DDx's internal profile_priority value.
  - DDx-internal vocabulary (profile_priority, profile_ladders, model_overrides) stays as-is per design — only the wire-boundary field name changes.
  - No subprocess pre-flight: trust Go-import-only scope; fix forward if DDx execs `fiz` subprocess somewhere we missed.

  - cli/tools/lint/{runtimelint,routinglint}/analyzer.go (string-match rules pinning old field names)
  - go.mod (pin to fizeau v0.10.x → v0.11.0)

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Bump go.mod fizeau pin to the released v0.11.0 tag.
